sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.54 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: A distance-regular graph of diameter 3 with the second eigenvalue$_1=a_3$is called a . For a Shilla graph$$the number$a=a_3$divides$k$and we set$b=b( )=k/a$. Koolen and Park proved that there are 12 feasible intersection arrays of Shilla graphs with$b=3$. At present it is proved that a Shilla graph with$b=3$has intersection array$\{12,10,3;1,3,8\}$(Doro graph),$\{12,10,5;1,1,8\}$(nonisotropic unit...
